response.justification: The candidate has negligible C++ experience — approximately six months writing C/C++ components at National Instruments in 2014-2015 — against a hard minimum requirement of eight years. C++ does not appear in his skills section at all. The entire technical core of this role (C++-based cloud compute platform, systems-level infrastructure) is absent from his background, which skews heavily toward TypeScript/Node.js web services, DLang, and product engineering. While Bogdan shows genuine depth in software craftsmanship and distributed service work, those strengths cannot compensate for the fundamental language and domain mismatch.
strengths: Consistent application of software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing) across multiple roles at scale
Real distributed-systems exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O
Staff-level influence demonstrated through public talks, multi-day workshops, open-source infrastructure projects (GISCollective on Kubernetes), and recurring internal Refactoring Dojos
concerns: C++ experience is approximately six months in 2014; the role's minimum threshold is eight years — a disqualifying gap
No evidence of cloud compute platform ownership, hyperscaler infrastructure work, or production cloud architecture at the depth required by the JD
No sustained technical team leadership; mentoring is limited to workshops and peer coaching rather than accountable lead ownership over engineering teams or multi-team alignment
